judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations; Report 1 is slightly stronger for correctly identifying that GitHub's PR view is the three-dot diff (Report 2 wrongly frames the two-dot diff as the PR view), but they are largely comparable.

report_1.evidence: `main`. Per project documentation's dead-base rule, every PR should be based on the trunk, not on another feature branch. `feat/pairwise-metric` was in fact built on top of `feat/weighted-scorer` (see Q2), but that base branch is a dead base now — so the PR must not target `feat/weighted-scorer`, it must target `main` directly

report_2.evidence: `main` (SHA `1971f08...`). `feat/weighted-scorer` — the branch `feat/pairwise-metric` was actually built on — is a dead end: its content has already landed on `main` (see Q2), so there is nothing left to target there. Targeting `main`

report_1.evidence: `feat/pairwise-metric` branches from `ac9d505` (`W3`), the tip of `feat/weighted-scorer`... `feat/weighted-scorer`'s own three commits ... are **not** an ancestor of `main` (`git merge-base --is-ancestor feat/weighted-scorer main` → NO). Yet `main` already contains equivalent functionality via a single commit, `1971f08 "Refactor scorer for weighted metrics (#42)"` ... This is a squash/rewrite merge ... the original `feat/weighted-scorer` branch/commits are orphaned ... `feat/weighted-scorer` still exists as a ref

report_2.evidence: `feat/pairwise-metric` was branched from the tip of `feat/weighted-scorer` (commit `ac9d505`) ... `git merge-base --is-ancestor ac9d505 main` returns **NO** — `feat/weighted-scorer`'s commits are not ancestors of `main`. ... PR #42's single commit `1971f08` ... This is the signature of a **squash merge** ... history-wise it is orphaned

report_1.evidence: Not all of that is pairwise-specific — some is a byproduct of history: ... The rest — the `overlap` helper and the `scorer.py` restructuring into weighted totals — is leftover W1–W3 (`feat/weighted-scorer`) content. It shows up small/clean in the three-dot diff only because `main`'s independent refactor (`1971f08`) happens to have produced byte-identical file content for `scorer.py`

report_2.evidence: The earlier three-dot diff (`main...feat/pairwise-metric`, 21 lines across 3 files) was misleading — it diffs from the merge-base (`65204ad`, the pre-refactor base), so it also showed the `overlap`/`scorer.py`-weighting changes. Those aren't new content relative to `main`'s current tip; they're already there under a different commit hash (`1971f08`) due to the squash merge.

report_1.evidence: rebase ... `feat/pairwise-metric` so only the two pairwise-specific commits (`X1` ... and `X2` ...) are replayed on top of `origin/main`'s tip (`1971f08`), dropping `W1`/`W2`/`W3` ... After rebasing, re-verify: `git merge-base --is-ancestor main feat/pairwise-metric` should be YES, and `git diff main...feat/pairwise-metric` should show only the true pairwise-specific delta ... with no incidental weighted-scorer leftovers.

report_2.evidence: **Rebase `feat/pairwise-metric` onto `main`** (not merge) — e.g. `git rebase --onto main feat/weighted-scorer feat/pairwise-metric` ... **Verify post-rebase diff** is exactly the 2-file/5-line change identified in Q3 (`git diff main..feat/pairwise-metric`), confirming no reintroduction of the weighted-scorer content.
